channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Global Development Public Policy Youth Innovation Contest 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for Young Changemakers
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The Global Development Public Policy Youth Innovation Contest 2026 is now open for applications, offering young people worldwide a unique platform to transform their ideas into impactful public policy solutions. This global initiative provides participants with the opportunity to engage with international experts, gain global recognition, and contribute to shaping policies that promote cultural diversity. […]

The post Global Development Public Policy Youth Innovation Contest 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for Young Changemakers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Global Development Public Policy Youth Innovation Contest 2026 is now open for applications, offering young people worldwide a unique platform to transform their ideas into impactful public policy solutions. This global initiative provides participants with the opportunity to engage with international experts, gain global recognition, and contribute to shaping policies that promote cultural diversity.

With the 2026 theme focused on Preserving Cultural Diversity, this contest invites innovative youth-led solutions that address one of the most pressing global development priorities.

About the Youth Innovation Contest 2026

The Global Development Public Policy Youth Innovation Contest is designed to empower young leaders to develop practical and scalable policy solutions. Participants will work individually or in teams to propose ideas that can influence global development discussions and reach key international institutions.

Selected finalists will receive financial support to travel to China and present their ideas at a high-level global platform.

Theme: Preserving Cultural Diversity

Participants are required to develop policy proposals that address challenges and opportunities related to cultural diversity. This may include topics linked to:

  • Cultural preservation and heritage protection

  • Inclusion and social cohesion

  • Indigenous knowledge systems

  • Education and cultural awareness

  • Sustainable development and the SDGs

  • Digital innovation and cultural expression

Who Can Apply

This opportunity is open to a wide range of young people globally. Eligible applicants include:

  • Young individuals from all countries and backgrounds

  • Students, graduates, and early-career professionals

  • Individuals interested in public policy, international development, and cultural issues

  • Youth motivated to create real-world impact through policy innovation

Participants may apply:

  • Individually, or

  • As a team of up to five members

What You Need to Do

To participate in the contest, applicants must complete the following steps:

  • Select a topic related to cultural diversity

  • Develop a detailed public policy proposal in English

  • Complete the application form

  • Submit both the application and full proposal before the deadline

Benefits of the Youth Innovation Contest

Selected participants and finalists will gain access to a wide range of benefits, including:

  • Opportunity for your work to reach United Nations institutions

  • Recommendation letter for a United Nations internship

  • Invitation to pitch your idea at UNESCO during the Cultural Exchange Forum (June 10, 2026)

  • Travel support to China for selected finalists

  • International exposure and recognition

  • Academic and policy-level visibility

  • Networking opportunities with global youth leaders and experts

Why You Should Apply

This contest is more than just a competition—it is a pathway to global influence. Participants will not only develop policy skills but also gain exposure to international platforms that can accelerate their careers in development, governance, and global policy.

Whether you are passionate about cultural preservation, social inclusion, or sustainable development, this opportunity allows you to turn your ideas into actionable solutions with global relevance.

How to Apply

To apply for the Global Development Public Policy Youth Innovation Contest 2026:

  • Click here for more info

  • Prepare your completed application form

  • Develop your full policy proposal in English

  • Ensure you meet all requirements outlined in the official guidelines

  • Submit your complete application via email

Application Email: contact@gydca.org

Application Deadline

  • Deadline: April 15, 2026

Late or incomplete applications may not be considered, so early submission is strongly encouraged.

Full details here https://qr61.cn/oeWlMx/qgQuERK?lid=05so8knvt4zftc4j1h&rlid=05so8knvt4zftc4j1h

The Global Development Public Policy Youth Innovation Contest 2026 is a powerful opportunity for young changemakers to influence global conversations on cultural diversity. By participating, you position yourself at the intersection of policy, innovation, and global development.

If you have an idea that can shape the future, this is your chance to share it with the world.

The post Global Development Public Policy Youth Innovation Contest 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for Young Changemakers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: World Bank Group (WBG) Youth Summit 2026 Speaker Applications Now Open: Shape the Future of Jobs in the Digital Age
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The World Bank Group (WBG) Youth Summit 2026 is now inviting applications for speakers to join one of the most influential global platforms for youth-led development dialogue. If you are a young leader, practitioner, entrepreneur, or researcher working on employment, innovation, or inclusive development, this is your opportunity to contribute to shaping global conversations. About […]

The post World Bank Group (WBG) Youth Summit 2026 Speaker Applications Now Open: Shape the Future of Jobs in the Digital Age appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The World Bank Group (WBG) Youth Summit 2026 is now inviting applications for speakers to join one of the most influential global platforms for youth-led development dialogue. If you are a young leader, practitioner, entrepreneur, or researcher working on employment, innovation, or inclusive development, this is your opportunity to contribute to shaping global conversations.

About the WBG Youth Summit

The WBG Youth Summit is the World Bank’s largest annual event dedicated to young people aged 18–35. It brings together emerging leaders, experts, and changemakers from around the world to explore practical solutions to global development challenges.

The Summit features:

  • High-level plenary sessions with global experts

  • Interactive workshops and hands-on labs

  • A pitch competition for innovative ideas

  • Networking opportunities with World Bank staff and partners

Theme for 2026: FutureWorks – Designing Jobs for the Digital Age

The 2026 Summit focuses on a critical global challenge: creating sustainable and inclusive employment opportunities for the 1.2 billion young people expected to enter the workforce over the next decade.

The agenda will explore how innovation, policy, and investment can come together to generate quality jobs in a rapidly changing world.

Key Focus Areas

The Summit will center on the following thematic areas:

  • Education and Skills Development

  • Entrepreneurship and Job Creation

  • Agriculture and Agribusiness

  • Technology and Innovation

  • Development Financing

  • Fragility, Conflict, and Violence

Speakers are expected to provide practical, action-oriented insights that demonstrate how these sectors can contribute to inclusive and sustainable employment for youth.

Call for Speakers: Who Should Apply

The Youth Summit Committee is seeking individuals who can contribute meaningful perspectives and real-world solutions. Ideal candidates include:

  • Young professionals and emerging leaders

  • Entrepreneurs and startup founders

  • Researchers and academics

  • Development practitioners and policy experts

  • Innovators working in digital, agricultural, or financial sectors

Applicants should have demonstrated experience or ideas related to job creation, digital transformation, or youth economic empowerment.

Why You Should Apply

Selected speakers will have the opportunity to:

  • Share their work on a global stage

  • Influence conversations on youth employment and development

  • Connect with international organizations, policymakers, and innovators

  • Contribute to shaping solutions for the future of work

How to Apply

Interested applicants can submit their speaker nominations through the official application form:

  • Apply here: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScuLkpCPPg6UZxDq0kqaW5gQL9Rgs_zeTQzdAkKpVUlCACwzg/viewform

Applicants are encouraged to clearly articulate their expertise, experience, and the unique insights they can bring to the Summit.

Application Deadline

  • Deadline to apply: 1 May 2026

About the World Bank Group Youth Summit

The Youth Summit is a flagship initiative of the World Bank Group aimed at amplifying youth voices in global development. It serves as a platform for collaboration, innovation, and knowledge exchange, helping to bridge the gap between young leaders and global decision-makers.

  • Learn more: https://www.worldbank.org/youthsummit

  • Apply here: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScuLkpCPPg6UZxDq0kqaW5gQL9Rgs_zeTQzdAkKpVUlCACwzg/viewform

If you are working on solutions that address employment, digital transformation, or inclusive growth, this is a unique opportunity to contribute to a global dialogue that drives real impact. Apply now and be part of shaping the future of work for the next generation.

The post World Bank Group (WBG) Youth Summit 2026 Speaker Applications Now Open: Shape the Future of Jobs in the Digital Age appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Reham Al-Farra Memorial Journalism Fellowship 2026 (RAF) – Fully Funded UN Programme
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Reham Al-Farra (RAF) Memorial Journalism Fellowship 2026, a prestigious United Nations programme designed for young professional journalists from developing countries and economies in transition. The application period runs until 3 April 2026. Applicants are strongly encouraged to review the official FAQ before submitting their application. About the RAF Journalism […]

The post Reham Al-Farra Memorial Journalism Fellowship 2026 (RAF) – Fully Funded UN Programme appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Reham Al-Farra (RAF) Memorial Journalism Fellowship 2026, a prestigious United Nations programme designed for young professional journalists from developing countries and economies in transition.

The application period runs until 3 April 2026.

Applicants are strongly encouraged to review the official FAQ before submitting their application.

About the RAF Journalism Fellowship

The Reham Al-Farra Memorial Journalism Fellowship was established by the United Nations General Assembly in December 1980 under Resolution 35/201. The programme was originally known as the DPI Training Programme for Broadcasters and Journalists from Developing Countries.

In 2003, the fellowship was renamed in memory of Reham Al-Farra, a 29-year-old United Nations Public Information Officer who was killed during the bombing of the UN headquarters in Baghdad on 19 August 2003.

Since its launch in 1981, the fellowship has supported nearly 700 journalists from 168 countries, contributing to improved global understanding of the United Nations and its work.

Programme Overview

The RAF Fellowship is a highly competitive opportunity that enables selected journalists to gain first-hand experience covering the United Nations.

During the programme, fellows will:

  • Cover the United Nations General Assembly

  • Attend high-level UN briefings and sessions

  • Interview senior UN officials

  • Engage with UN agencies, funds, and programmes

  • Meet Permanent Representatives and diplomats

  • Participate in training sessions with media experts

  • Connect with journalists and media organizations in New York

The programme provides a unique platform to strengthen professional skills, expand networks, and enhance global reporting capacity.

Programme Duration

The 2026 RAF Fellowship is expected to take place:

  • Over a four-week period

  • Between September and October 2026

  • In a format that may be virtual or hybrid (to be confirmed)

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Be between 22 and 35 years old

  • Be a full-time working journalist

  • Have at least two years of professional journalism experience

  • Be a national of an eligible developing country or economy in transition

  • Demonstrate experience covering UN-related issues or global topics

Additional considerations:

  • Freelance journalists are eligible, provided they submit strong work samples and proof of professional accreditation

  • Applicants who are studying or teaching journalism but are not working full-time as journalists are not eligible

Applicants may live outside their country of citizenship, provided they are working as journalists covering UN-related issues relevant to their home country.

Eligible Countries

The fellowship is open to journalists from a wide range of developing countries and countries with economies in transition across:

  • Africa

  • Asia

  • Latin America and the Caribbean

  • Middle East

  • Eastern Europe and Central Asia

The full list includes countries such as Ghana, Nigeria, Kenya, South Africa, India, Indonesia, Brazil, Mexico, Egypt, Philippines, and many others.

Required Application Documents

Applicants must submit the following:

  • Completed online application form

  • Curriculum Vitae (CV) in English

  • At least five published work samples related to the United Nations or its priority issues (from the last six months)

  • Copy of passport or national identification

  • Two reference letters

All documents must be uploaded to a file-sharing platform such as Google Drive or Dropbox. Applicants must provide a link with permissions that allow reviewers to access and download all files.

Failure to submit all required materials may result in disqualification.

Submission Guidelines for Broadcast Journalists

Television and radio journalists should:

  • Provide links to publicly accessible content where possible

  • Upload media files to a cloud platform if links are unavailable

  • Ensure all files are accessible through shared links

Language Requirements

  • English proficiency is required, as it is the primary language of communication during the programme

  • Fellows may publish their work in their native languages

Reference letters do not need to be translated into English but must include complete contact details for verification.

Selection Criteria

Applications will be assessed based on:

  • Professional experience

  • Quality and relevance of work samples

  • Coverage of UN-related issues

  • Audience reach and impact

  • Geographic diversity

  • Gender balance

How to Apply

Applications must be submitted through the official online portal:

https://www.un.org/en/raf/application2026

Applicants are required to type their name in the declaration field to sign the application and ensure submission is completed successfully.

Application Deadline

  • Applications open: 16 February 2026

  • Application deadline: 3 April 2026

Why You Should Apply

The RAF Fellowship offers a unique opportunity to:

  • Gain direct exposure to international diplomacy and policymaking

  • Build relationships with global media professionals

  • Access high-level UN officials and decision-makers

  • Strengthen reporting skills on global issues

  • Contribute to improved public understanding of the United Nations

Apply here: https://www.un.org/en/raf/application2026

For more info visit : https://www.un.org/en/raf?utm_content=buffer792af&utm_medium=social&utm_source=twitter.com&utm_campaign=buffer

Explore More Opportunities

For more fellowships and journalism opportunities, visit:
https://opportunitiesforyouth.org/?s=fellowship

The post Reham Al-Farra Memorial Journalism Fellowship 2026 (RAF) – Fully Funded UN Programme appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Blue Ridge Labs Founder Fellowship 2026: Turn Bold Social Impact Ideas Into Fundable Ventures
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
Innovative ideas have the power to reshape societies, tackle inequality, and improve economic mobility. However, transforming a promising concept into a sustainable venture often requires the right combination of mentorship, funding, research support, and access to a strong professional network. To support emerging entrepreneurs who want to build impactful solutions, Blue Ridge Labs, the venture […]

The post Blue Ridge Labs Founder Fellowship 2026: Turn Bold Social Impact Ideas Into Fundable Ventures appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Innovative ideas have the power to reshape societies, tackle inequality, and improve economic mobility. However, transforming a promising concept into a sustainable venture often requires the right combination of mentorship, funding, research support, and access to a strong professional network.

To support emerging entrepreneurs who want to build impactful solutions, Blue Ridge Labs, the venture studio of Robin Hood Foundation, has opened applications for the 2026 Founder Fellowship. This program is designed to help early-stage founders transform bold ideas into minimum viable products (MVPs) and fundable ventures capable of addressing pressing social challenges.

The fellowship is structured to guide participants through the process of testing ideas, validating solutions with real users, and building technology-driven ventures that promote economic mobility and social change.

About Blue Ridge Labs

Blue Ridge Labs is a venture studio created by the Robin Hood Foundation, a major philanthropic organization focused on fighting poverty and increasing economic opportunity. Through its programs and venture-building initiatives, Blue Ridge Labs supports entrepreneurs working on innovative solutions to systemic social issues.

The Founder Fellowship is one of its flagship initiatives, helping mission-driven founders transform early ideas into scalable ventures that can attract funding and deliver meaningful impact.

A Fellowship Designed for Early-Stage Innovators

The Founder Fellowship specifically targets individuals who are passionate about solving social problems but may not yet have a fully developed startup.

Unlike many startup accelerators that require a prototype or existing product, this fellowship welcomes applicants at the idea stage. Participants only need a clear concept, a strong motivation to build, and the determination to transform their idea into a working venture.

The fellowship helps founders move through the critical early stages of entrepreneurship, including research, validation, product development, and initial fundraising preparation.

What Participants Will Gain

The Blue Ridge Labs Founder Fellowship provides an extensive range of resources designed to support founders during the earliest stages of venture creation.

Weekly Learning and Development Sessions

Participants join structured weekly sessions led by experienced founders, entrepreneurs, and industry professionals. These sessions cover key areas essential for startup success, including:

  • Customer research and problem validation

  • Product development strategies

  • Startup growth and traction building

  • Fundraising preparation and investor engagement

These sessions ensure that fellows gain practical knowledge and insights while actively building their ventures.

Direct User Feedback and Market Insights

A crucial step in building a successful product is understanding the needs of real users. Through the fellowship, participants gain access to the Design Insights Group (DIG) community, which provides valuable feedback and research insights.

This allows founders to test their ideas, refine their concepts, and ensure that the solutions they build address real-world needs.

Personalized Mentorship and Expert Guidance

Fellows benefit from continuous support through:

  • One-on-one mentorship

  • Group coaching sessions

  • Hands-on technical assistance

Experts in engineering, product design, and entrepreneurship provide guidance throughout the program, helping founders overcome technical and strategic challenges.

$20,000 Stipend to Build Your Venture

One of the most valuable aspects of the fellowship is the $20,000 stipend provided to each participant.

This financial support allows founders to dedicate focused time to developing their ideas, conducting research, and building their MVP without immediate financial pressure.

Collaborative Workspace in Brooklyn

During the in-person stage of the program, fellows work alongside other entrepreneurs at the Blue Ridge Labs workspace in Brooklyn, New York City.

This collaborative environment encourages creativity, peer learning, and partnerships among founders who share a commitment to building solutions for social impact.

Access to a Powerful Network

Fellows will also have the opportunity to showcase their ventures to leaders across multiple sectors, including:

  • Technology

  • Philanthropy

  • Public policy

Through the network of the Robin Hood Foundation, participants gain exposure to influential stakeholders who can help accelerate their ventures.

Program Structure: A 20-Week Journey From Idea to Venture

The Founder Fellowship follows a structured roadmap designed to guide participants through the early stages of venture development.

Step 1: Explore and Test (Virtual Phase)

The program begins with a remote phase where fellows explore their ideas, conduct research, and test their assumptions with potential users. During this stage, participants focus on validating their concept and identifying the real needs of their target audience.

Step 2: Build Your MVP (In-Person Phase)

In the second stage, fellows move into an in-person environment at the Blue Ridge Labs space in Brooklyn. Here, participants work intensively to build their minimum viable product and prepare their venture for potential investment.

By the end of the 20-week fellowship, founders will have transformed their concept into a validated product prototype and a venture ready for further development or funding.

Who Should Apply

The fellowship is ideal for individuals who:

  • Have an idea for a social impact technology venture

  • Want to address challenges related to economic mobility and social equity

  • Are passionate about turning ideas into real solutions

  • Are ready to commit to a structured venture-building program

Applicants do not need a prototype, technical background, or co-founder to apply. The fellowship is designed to help founders move from idea to MVP through structured support.

Why This Fellowship Matters

Many of the world’s most transformative startups began as simple ideas driven by individuals determined to solve real problems. However, without access to mentorship, funding, and networks, promising ideas often struggle to reach their full potential.

The Blue Ridge Labs Founder Fellowship bridges this gap by providing the resources, community, and financial support that early-stage founders need to build ventures capable of creating lasting social impact.

For entrepreneurs passionate about addressing inequality and improving economic opportunity, this fellowship represents a powerful opportunity to turn a bold idea into a scalable solution.

Deadline

The application deadline is May 3, 2026.

Applicants interested in joining the 2026 cohort, which begins in June 2026, are encouraged to submit their applications before the deadline.

For more information click here 

Apply here 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post Blue Ridge Labs Founder Fellowship 2026: Turn Bold Social Impact Ideas Into Fundable Ventures appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Powerful Max Kampelman Fellowship 2026 for Graduate Students in Washington DC
item.dc:creator: Michelle Zyongwe
item.description:
The Powerful Max Kampelman Fellowship 2026 offers an outstanding opportunity for graduate students and recent graduates interested in international affairs, human rights, and security policy to gain practical experience working at a U.S. government commission in Washington, DC. This highly respected fellowship program prepares emerging leaders to engage with complex global challenges while contributing […]

The post Powerful Max Kampelman Fellowship 2026 for Graduate Students in Washington DC appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Powerful Max Kampelman Fellowship 2026 offers an outstanding opportunity for graduate students and recent graduates interested in international affairs, human rights, and security policy to gain practical experience working at a U.S. government commission in Washington, DC. This highly respected fellowship program prepares emerging leaders to engage with complex global challenges while contributing to policy discussions related to human rights, security cooperation, and economic collaboration.

The fellowship is hosted by the U.S. Commission on Security and Cooperation in Europe, also known as the Helsinki Commission. Named after longtime U.S. Ambassador Max Kampelman, the fellowship recognizes and develops the next generation of leaders working in security policy, diplomacy, and strategic communications.

The commission plays a vital role in advancing national security by promoting cooperation and stability among 57 countries across Europe, Eurasia, and North America through initiatives related to human rights, economic cooperation, and military security.

Applications for the Summer 2026 Max Kampelman Fellowship are currently open and will close on March 29, 2026.

Max Kampelman Fellowship Overview and Work Experience

The Max Kampelman Fellowship provides participants with the opportunity to work alongside experienced policymakers, researchers, and communications specialists at the Helsinki Commission. Fellows contribute directly to policy discussions and research activities that influence international security cooperation and human rights policy.

The fellowship lasts three months, with fellows working approximately 30 hours per week in person at the commission’s office in Washington, DC. Fellows receive $27 per hour for their work, along with access to professional development opportunities, mentorship from senior commission staff, and networking events with policy professionals.

During their fellowship period, participants gain firsthand experience observing how U.S. congressional commissions operate and how international policy initiatives are developed and implemented.

Max Kampelman Policy Fellowship Opportunities

Participants applying for the Policy Fellowship track will work closely with commission policy advisors focusing on issues related to political and military affairs, economic and environmental matters, and human rights and fundamental freedoms.

Policy fellows contribute to research and policy analysis covering developments throughout the Organization for Security and Co-operation in Europe (OSCE) region, which includes the 57 participating countries across Europe, Eurasia, and North America.

Typical responsibilities for policy fellows include researching emerging political, economic, and human rights trends, assisting with congressional hearings and briefings, supporting legislative initiatives, and contributing to policy publications. Fellows may also attend congressional hearings, policy events, and international briefings relevant to the commission’s work.

Each policy fellow is expected to produce at least one research article or policy piece during the fellowship period that may be published on the commission’s website.

Max Kampelman Communications Fellowship Opportunities

The Communications Fellowship track focuses on public engagement, media outreach, and strategic communications related to the commission’s work.

Communications fellows support various projects that help raise public awareness of the commission’s initiatives. Fellows assist with promoting hearings and briefings, managing media outreach, developing digital content, and supporting social media communications.

Responsibilities may also include drafting press materials, preparing content for the commission’s website, assisting with event communications, and supporting multimedia storytelling initiatives.

Like policy fellows, communications fellows are also expected to produce one article or content piece during their fellowship that may be published on the commission’s website.

Max Kampelman Professional Development and Networking

One of the most valuable aspects of the Max Kampelman Fellowship is the opportunity to work within a professional policy environment in Washington, DC. Fellows gain exposure to high-level discussions on international security, human rights, and diplomacy.

The program also provides mentorship from experienced policy advisors and communications professionals who guide fellows throughout the fellowship period. These mentorship opportunities help fellows strengthen their research, writing, and policy analysis skills while building valuable professional networks.

Fellows are encouraged to attend policy events, panel discussions, congressional hearings, and other public forums that provide deeper insight into international policy issues.

This immersive experience provides participants with the practical knowledge and professional connections needed to pursue careers in government, international organizations, research institutions, and policy advocacy.

Max Kampelman Application Process

Applicants interested in the Max Kampelman Fellowship 2026 must submit a complete application package by email.

The application package should include a brief cover letter explaining the applicant’s interest in working for the commission and describing relevant academic or professional experiences. Applicants should also indicate their availability, including preferred start and end dates and expected weekly working hours.

Applicants must also submit a resume limited to two pages and a writing sample of no more than three pages. Communications fellowship applicants may optionally include samples of creative work such as copywriting, graphic design, photography, or multimedia projects.

Applications must be sent to the official fellowship email address provided by the commission. Applicants should include their first initial and last name in the email subject line, along with the fellowship track they are applying for.

Only complete applications submitted before the official deadline will be considered.

Deadline for Applications

The deadline to apply for the Summer 2026 Max Kampelman Fellowship is March 29, 2026.

Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications well before the deadline to ensure their materials are reviewed.

Learn More About This Opportunity

To learn more about the Powerful Max Kampelman Fellowship 2026 and apply for the program, visit the official application page here.

Conclusion

The Max Kampelman Fellowship 2026 provides an exceptional opportunity for graduate students and recent graduates interested in international policy, security affairs, and human rights to gain meaningful professional experience within a U.S. government commission. By working alongside policy experts and communications professionals, fellows gain valuable insights into international diplomacy, congressional policy processes, and global security cooperation.

Participants leave the program with stronger research and policy skills, expanded professional networks, and practical experience that can launch successful careers in international affairs and public policy.

For more fellowship opportunities on OFY Opportunities click here

The post Powerful Max Kampelman Fellowship 2026 for Graduate Students in Washington DC appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Empowering Young Entrepreneurs in Kenya: Apply for the GIZ Accelerator Programme for Youth-Led Businesses
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
Young entrepreneurs play a crucial role in driving innovation, economic growth, and job creation. Across Kenya, youth-led enterprises are transforming local industries by introducing creative solutions, strengthening value chains, and building sustainable businesses that benefit their communities. However, many early-stage businesses face common challenges, including limited access to finance, inadequate management training, and insufficient professional […]

The post Empowering Young Entrepreneurs in Kenya: Apply for the GIZ Accelerator Programme for Youth-Led Businesses appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young entrepreneurs play a crucial role in driving innovation, economic growth, and job creation. Across Kenya, youth-led enterprises are transforming local industries by introducing creative solutions, strengthening value chains, and building sustainable businesses that benefit their communities. However, many early-stage businesses face common challenges, including limited access to finance, inadequate management training, and insufficient professional networks.

To address these barriers and support the next generation of business leaders, the German development agency Deutsche Gesellschaft für Internationale Zusammenarbeit (GIZ) in Kenya, in collaboration with Kenya Institute of Business Training and the Youth Enterprise Development Fund, has launched a dedicated accelerator programme designed to empower youth entrepreneurs. This initiative focuses on strengthening business management capabilities, improving financial access, and creating opportunities for sustainable growth among young innovators operating in key sectors of Kenya’s economy.

Supporting Youth-Led Businesses in Key Kenyan Counties

This accelerator programme specifically targets entrepreneurs operating in Kiambu County, Machakos County, Kajiado County, and Nairobi County. These regions represent some of the most vibrant entrepreneurial hubs in Kenya, where young people are establishing businesses that contribute to both local and national economic development.

Entrepreneurs selected for the programme will receive comprehensive support aimed at helping them scale their operations, improve operational efficiency, and strengthen their competitive advantage in the market.

Focus Sectors: Strengthening High-Impact Industries

The accelerator programme is designed to support businesses operating within sectors that have strong potential for economic transformation and employment creation. Eligible enterprises must be involved in one of the following areas:

Agro-Processing and Food Value Addition

Agro-processing plays a critical role in enhancing agricultural productivity and reducing post-harvest losses. Businesses working in food value addition—such as packaging, preservation, processing, or transforming raw agricultural products into higher-value goods—are encouraged to apply. By strengthening these enterprises, the programme aims to improve food systems and create new market opportunities for farmers and processors.

Leather Processing and Value Addition

Kenya possesses a strong livestock sector, creating significant potential for the leather industry. Businesses focused on leather processing, product development, and value addition—such as leather goods manufacturing—are vital for strengthening domestic production and reducing reliance on imported finished products.

Textile Processing and Value Addition

The textile sector continues to offer strong opportunities for youth entrepreneurship, particularly in design, production, and value-added manufacturing. Enterprises that process textiles or develop finished textile products contribute to local industry growth and promote job creation.

Programme Objectives: Accelerating Sustainable Growth

The accelerator programme aims to empower young entrepreneurs through a structured approach that addresses several key areas essential for business success.

Strengthening Business Management Skills

Participants will gain access to targeted training and mentorship designed to enhance their leadership and business management capabilities. The programme helps entrepreneurs develop strategic planning skills, improve operational systems, and adopt sustainable growth strategies.

Expanding Professional Networks and Linkages

Entrepreneurial success often depends on access to strong professional networks. Through this programme, participants will have opportunities to connect with industry experts, mentors, financial institutions, and fellow entrepreneurs. These connections can lead to valuable partnerships and collaborative opportunities.

Improving Access to Finance

Many early-stage businesses struggle to secure funding for expansion. The accelerator programme helps entrepreneurs better understand financing options, prepare investment-ready proposals, and connect with financial institutions that support youth-led enterprises.

Accelerating Business Growth

Ultimately, the goal of the programme is to help participants scale their businesses sustainably. By combining training, mentorship, and networking opportunities, the initiative provides the tools and resources needed to help entrepreneurs grow their enterprises and strengthen their market presence.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Entrepreneurship among young people is one of the most powerful drivers of economic transformation in Africa. By investing in youth-led businesses, programmes like this accelerator contribute to:

  • Job creation and economic inclusion

  • Strengthening local value chains

  • Promoting innovation and sustainable industries

  • Supporting long-term economic resilience

Young entrepreneurs often possess the creativity, energy, and vision needed to transform traditional industries. With the right support systems in place, their businesses can evolve into powerful engines of economic growth.

Who Should Apply

The programme is designed for entrepreneurs who are ready to grow their businesses and take them to the next level. Applicants should:

  • Be between 18 and 35 years old

  • Operate a business that is less than seven years old

  • Run enterprises located in Kiambu, Machakos, Kajiado, or Nairobi counties

  • Operate in agro-processing, leather processing, or textile value addition sectors

Entrepreneurs who are passionate about innovation, sustainability, and business growth are particularly encouraged to apply.

How This Accelerator Can Transform Your Business

Participating in an accelerator programme can be a turning point for many businesses. Beyond training and mentorship, accelerators provide exposure, credibility, and opportunities that can significantly accelerate growth.

For young entrepreneurs in Kenya, this programme represents a chance to gain the knowledge, networks, and financial insights necessary to strengthen their enterprises and compete more effectively in the market.

If you are a young business owner seeking to scale your enterprise and expand your impact, this accelerator programme offers a unique opportunity to gain the tools and support needed for long-term success.

Deadline

No specific application deadline has been announced yet. Interested applicants are encouraged to apply as soon as possible once the application portal is open.

Apply here 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post Empowering Young Entrepreneurs in Kenya: Apply for the GIZ Accelerator Programme for Youth-Led Businesses appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Agribusiness e-Academy Innovation Challenge 2026: Win €2,000 Funding, Mentorship, or Tech Support for Climate-Smart Agribusiness Ideas
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The Agribusiness e-Academy Innovation Challenge 2026 (Window 2) is a powerful opportunity for young and emerging agripreneurs to transform their ideas into viable, climate-smart businesses. Designed and implemented by the Agri-Business Facility for Africa (ABF) in collaboration with GIZ and international partners, this initiative provides a free, structured learning pathway that equips participants with the […]

The post Agribusiness e-Academy Innovation Challenge 2026: Win €2,000 Funding, Mentorship, or Tech Support for Climate-Smart Agribusiness Ideas appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Agribusiness e-Academy Innovation Challenge 2026 (Window 2) is a powerful opportunity for young and emerging agripreneurs to transform their ideas into viable, climate-smart businesses. Designed and implemented by the Agri-Business Facility for Africa (ABF) in collaboration with GIZ and international partners, this initiative provides a free, structured learning pathway that equips participants with the skills, tools, and knowledge required to build investment-ready agribusiness concepts.

In addition to gaining high-quality training, participants stand a chance to win €2,000 in funding, mentorship support, or a tablet/laptop, making this program a unique blend of learning, innovation, and opportunity.

About the Agribusiness e-Academy Innovation Challenge

The Agribusiness e-Academy is designed to address a critical gap in Africa’s entrepreneurial ecosystem: limited access to funding for early-stage agribusinesses. Statistics show that only a small percentage of startups secure external funding due to weak business models, lack of financial clarity, and insufficient climate-smart planning.

This initiative directly tackles these challenges by offering:

  • A step-by-step online learning pathway

  • Practical tools for building investment-ready agribusinesses

  • Exposure to climate-smart agricultural practices

  • Opportunities to pitch and win funding or support

Why This Opportunity Matters

The Agribusiness e-Academy Innovation Challenge is designed to help participants:

  • Build strong business logic and financial viability

  • Develop climate-resilient agribusiness strategies

  • Increase their chances of securing investment

  • Transform ideas into scalable and sustainable enterprises

This is particularly important in Africa, where agribusiness plays a key role in economic development, employment, and food security.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Be 18 years or older

  • Be based in a member state of the Organisation of African, Caribbean and Pacific States (OACPS)

  • Have an agribusiness idea related to:

    • Cocoa

    • Maize

    • Cashew

    • Livestock (cattle, sheep, goats)

This ensures that the program targets high-impact agricultural value chains.

Learning Pathway: Step-by-Step Breakdown

Participants must complete a structured learning journey consisting of the following courses:

1. Business Model Canvas for Agribusinesses

  • Learn how to design and structure a business model

  • Understand costs, pricing, and revenue streams

2. Climate-Smart Agribusiness: Adaptation

  • Identify climate risks within your value chain

  • Develop risk mitigation and adaptation strategies

3. Creating an Investment-Ready Agribusiness

  • Assess business viability

  • Prepare your idea for investors and funding opportunities

4. Develop Your AgriCanvas

  • Apply all learning into a Business Model Canvas tailored to your idea

  • Transform your concept into an investment-ready proposal

ACCESS ALL YOUR COURSES HERE

What You Can Win

Participants who successfully complete the program and submit their AgriCanvas stand a chance to win:

  • €2,000 in funding

  • Mentorship support from experts

  • A tablet or laptop

These rewards are designed to support the growth and implementation of innovative agribusiness ideas.

How to Apply: Step-by-Step Guide

Follow these steps carefully to submit a valid application:

Step 1: Register on atingi

  • Create a free account on the atingi platform

  • Access all required courses

Step 2: Complete Mandatory Courses

  • Business Model Canvas for Agribusinesses

  • Climate-Smart Agribusiness: Adaptation

  • Creating an Investment-Ready Agribusiness

Note: The third course unlocks only after completing the first two.

Step 3: Complete Your AgriCanvas

  • Use the official Business Model Canvas template

  • Clearly structure your agribusiness idea

Step 4: Submit Your Idea

  • Fill out the Official Submission Form

  • Enter your AgriCanvas directly (no uploads allowed)

  • Provide your certificate ID as proof of course completion

Important Submission Requirements

To ensure your application is considered:

  • Only submissions via the official form will be reviewed

  • A valid certificate ID is mandatory

  • AgriCanvas must be entered directly into the form

  • Only one submission per applicant is allowed

Application Deadline

All submissions must be completed by:

31 May 2026

Late or incomplete submissions will not be considered.

Selection and Evaluation Process

Applications will be assessed based on:

  • Business viability and clarity

  • Climate-smart innovation

  • Financial sustainability

  • Impact potential

  • Alignment with eligible value chains

Winners will be announced around September 2026 and contacted via email.

Learn more about the Agribusiness e-Academy Innovation Challenge 2026 HERE.

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Who can enter this challenge?

Young and emerging agripreneurs aged 18+ from OACPS member states.

Do I need to pay to participate?

No. The program and all courses are completely free.

Can I submit more than one idea?

No. Only one submission per applicant is allowed per window.

Do I need to complete all courses?

Yes. All three mandatory courses must be completed to qualify.

What happens if I am not selected?

You can improve your idea and reapply in future submission windows.

Can I participate if I have taken other courses?

Yes, but you must complete the required three courses.

How will winners be selected?

An expert committee will evaluate submissions based on predefined criteria.

What if my idea is outside the listed value chains?

It will not be considered. Only cocoa, maize, cashew, and livestock ideas are eligible.

How will I know if I am selected?

Winners will be contacted via email linked to their atingi account.

Click Here to Download the Terms & Conditions Document for Application

Discover other global Grant and Competition opportunities on OFY.

The post Agribusiness e-Academy Innovation Challenge 2026: Win €2,000 Funding, Mentorship, or Tech Support for Climate-Smart Agribusiness Ideas appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: 🌍 Future Leaders Programme 14 at SOAS: Industrial Policy for Structural Transformation in the Global South (Apply by 20 April 2026)
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
The Future Leaders Programme (FLP 14), hosted by the Development Leadership Dialogue (DLD) at SOAS University of London, is a prestigious, high-level leadership workshop designed for experienced professionals shaping the future of economic development in the Global South. This intensive programme brings together emerging leaders from across sectors to explore industrial policy, structural transformation, and […]

The post 🌍 Future Leaders Programme 14 at SOAS: Industrial Policy for Structural Transformation in the Global South (Apply by 20 April 2026) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Future Leaders Programme (FLP 14), hosted by the Development Leadership Dialogue (DLD) at SOAS University of London, is a prestigious, high-level leadership workshop designed for experienced professionals shaping the future of economic development in the Global South.

This intensive programme brings together emerging leaders from across sectors to explore industrial policy, structural transformation, and sustainable development strategies in a rapidly evolving global landscape.

📌 Programme Overview

The Future Leaders Programme is a flagship initiative of the Development Leadership Dialogue, co-directed by leading development economists including Ha-Joon Chang, Christopher Cramer, and Jonathan Di John.

The programme aims to bridge gaps between key development actors—governments, private sector, civil society, academia, and international organizations—through dialogue, collaboration, and shared learning.

Participants will engage in:

  • Expert-led lectures

  • Interactive discussions and debates

  • Real-world case studies

  • Peer-to-peer learning with global professionals

📅 Key Details

  • Programme Dates: 22–26 June 2026

  • Location: London, United Kingdom

  • Application Deadline: 20 April 2026

  • Programme Fee: £3,000

  • Scholarships: Limited full and partial funding available

🎯 Focus Area: Industrial Policy in the Global South

This year’s theme—Industrial Policy for Structural Transformation in the Global South—addresses one of the most critical challenges facing developing economies today.

Participants will explore:

1. Economic Transformation Challenges

  • Premature deindustrialization

  • Over-reliance on commodity exports

  • Low-productivity service sector growth

2. Climate and Sustainability Pressures

  • Balancing development with climate responsibility

  • Leveraging renewable energy opportunities

  • Addressing ecological degradation

3. Technology and Innovation

  • Digital transformation and AI

  • Technology transfer and global inequality

  • Investment in R&D and skills development

4. Global Economic Shifts

  • Changing geopolitical dynamics

  • South-South cooperation

  • Evolving trade and policy environments

🧠 What You Will Gain

Participating in FLP 14 offers:

  • Direct engagement with globally recognized experts and policymakers

  • Deep insights into industrial policy design and implementation

  • Exposure to real-world development challenges and solutions

  • A powerful network of high-level professionals across sectors

  • Practical tools for influencing policy and driving economic transformation

🎤 Confirmed Speakers

The programme features distinguished speakers from top institutions and policy backgrounds, including:

  • Antonio Andreoni (SOAS)

  • José Miguel Ahumada (University of Chile)

  • Andres Arauz (Former Minister, Ecuador)

  • Yemi Dipeolu (Former Economic Advisor, Nigeria)

  • Isabel Estevez (Mexico)

  • Jostein Hauge (University of Cambridge)

  • Verena Hitner (Brazil Ministry of Science)

  • Amir Lebdioui (University of Oxford)

  • Pamela Mondliwa (South Africa)

  • Richard Kozul-Wright (SOAS, former UNCTAD Chief Economist)

  • João Silva (University of Lisbon)

  • Yin Shao Loong (Malaysia)

📝 Application Process

Applicants must complete an online application form and provide:

  • A personal statement outlining their views on industrial policy challenges in the Global South

  • Evidence of relevant professional experience

  • Demonstration of leadership potential and future impact

Applicants seeking financial support must:

  • Clearly explain their financial situation

  • Specify the amount of funding required

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more short courses opportunities, click here. 

⚠️ Important Considerations

This is a highly selective and intensive programme with a small cohort. Applicants should carefully consider:

  • The total cost (programme fee + travel and accommodation)

  • The competitive selection process

  • The advanced level of discussion and participation required

🚀 Final Thoughts

The Future Leaders Programme 14 is a unique opportunity for experienced professionals to deepen their understanding of industrial policy while building a global network of changemakers.

If you are committed to shaping economic transformation in the Global South and ready to engage at a high level, this programme could be a powerful step forward in your leadership journey.

The post 🌍 Future Leaders Programme 14 at SOAS: Industrial Policy for Structural Transformation in the Global South (Apply by 20 April 2026) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: SheTrades Caribbean Business Catalogue 2026: A Powerful Global Opportunity for Women-Led Businesses in the Caribbean
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
Women entrepreneurs across the Caribbean now have a major opportunity to gain international visibility, connect with buyers, and attract investment through the SheTrades Caribbean Business Catalogue 2026. This initiative, led by the Caribbean Development Bank (CDB) in collaboration with the International Trade Centre (ITC), is a strategic platform designed to elevate women-led businesses (WLBs) into […]

The post SheTrades Caribbean Business Catalogue 2026: A Powerful Global Opportunity for Women-Led Businesses in the Caribbean appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Women entrepreneurs across the Caribbean now have a major opportunity to gain international visibility, connect with buyers, and attract investment through the SheTrades Caribbean Business Catalogue 2026. This initiative, led by the Caribbean Development Bank (CDB) in collaboration with the International Trade Centre (ITC), is a strategic platform designed to elevate women-led businesses (WLBs) into regional and global markets.

What Is the SheTrades Caribbean Business Catalogue?

The SheTrades Caribbean Business Catalogue is a regional showcase platform that highlights selected women-led businesses to a wide network of:

  • International buyers

  • Investors

  • Trade partners

  • Regional distributors

  • Global value chain actors

This catalogue is not just a directory—it is a strategic marketing and visibility tool designed to position Caribbean women entrepreneurs as competitive players in international trade.

By being featured, businesses gain a unique opportunity to:

  • Increase brand exposure across global markets

  • Attract partnerships and investment opportunities

  • Build credibility through association with recognized institutions

  • Expand export potential

Why This Opportunity Matters

Women-led businesses often face barriers such as limited access to markets, financing, and global networks. This initiative directly addresses those challenges by:

  • Providing international visibility

  • Enhancing market access opportunities

  • Supporting inclusive trade development

  • Strengthening regional economic participation of women entrepreneurs

The involvement of globally recognized organizations like the International Trade Centre ensures that selected businesses are positioned within a credible and influential trade ecosystem.

Who Can Apply?

This opportunity is specifically designed for:

Eligible Applicants:

  • Women-led businesses (WLBs)

  • Entrepreneurs operating within the Caribbean region

  • Businesses formally registered and operational

Geographic Eligibility:

Applicants must be based in Caribbean countries. While the exact country list is not specified, it typically includes nations within the Caribbean Community (CARICOM) and surrounding island states.

Mandatory Eligibility Requirement

To be considered for inclusion, applicants must:

  • Be a registered member of the SheTrades Caribbean Hub

If you are not yet registered, you must complete your membership registration before applying.

Application Requirements

Applicants are required to provide the following information:

  1. Legal Company Name

  2. Country of Registration

  3. Website or Social Media Handle (to be published in the catalogue)

  4. Primary Contact Email (will be publicly listed)

  5. Primary Contact Phone Number (will be publicly listed)

  6. Confirmation of SheTrades Caribbean Hub Membership

All submitted details will be used to build your business profile within the catalogue.

Apply here

Data Privacy and Confidentiality

Both the Caribbean Development Bank and the International Trade Centre emphasize strict confidentiality in handling applicant data.

  • Information provided is used solely for analytical and catalogue development purposes

  • Sensitive business data is protected and not misused

Benefits of Being Selected

Being included in the SheTrades Caribbean Business Catalogue offers multiple strategic advantages:

1. Global Exposure – Your business will be visible to international stakeholders actively seeking partnerships.

2. Increased Credibility – Association with reputable institutions boosts trust and brand reputation.

3. Market Expansion – Access new markets beyond your local or regional reach.

4. Networking Opportunities – Connect with buyers, investors, and trade facilitators.

5. Business Growth Potential – Unlock opportunities for scaling operations and increasing revenue streams.

Deadline

 30th March 2026

The SheTrades Caribbean Business Catalogue 2026 is more than just a listing—it is a gateway to global business growth, visibility, and opportunity for women entrepreneurs in the Caribbean.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post SheTrades Caribbean Business Catalogue 2026: A Powerful Global Opportunity for Women-Led Businesses in the Caribbean appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Urban Childcare Innovation Challenge 2026: Grants, Incubation & Mentorship for Women-Led Enterprises in India
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The Urban Childcare Innovation Challenge 2026 is a transformative initiative designed to reshape the childcare ecosystem in urban India. Led by UN Women India with support from the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) in India and implemented by Primus Partners Pvt. Ltd., this program seeks to identify, support, and scale innovative childcare solutions that empower […]

The post Urban Childcare Innovation Challenge 2026: Grants, Incubation & Mentorship for Women-Led Enterprises in India appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Urban Childcare Innovation Challenge 2026 is a transformative initiative designed to reshape the childcare ecosystem in urban India. Led by UN Women India with support from the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) in India and implemented by Primus Partners Pvt. Ltd., this program seeks to identify, support, and scale innovative childcare solutions that empower women and strengthen urban care systems.

Childcare remains one of the most critical barriers to women’s economic participation, particularly in low-income urban communities. This challenge directly addresses issues of accessibility, affordability, quality, and sustainability in childcare services by supporting enterprises that deliver innovative and scalable solutions.

About the Urban Childcare Innovation Challenge

The Urban Childcare Innovation Challenge is a structured incubation program that focuses on:

  • Supporting women-led and women-benefiting childcare enterprises

  • Promoting sustainable and scalable business models

  • Strengthening urban care systems

  • Reducing unpaid care burdens on women

  • Advancing women’s economic empowerment

Selected enterprises will undergo a 3-month incubation and mentorship program, gaining access to funding, networks, and technical expertise.

Objectives of the Initiative

The program is designed with clear, impact-driven objectives:

  1. Identify and support 8–10 high-potential childcare enterprises through a competitive selection process.

  2. Provide tailored incubation, mentorship, and capacity-building support.

  3. Offer technical guidance and financial assistance to build investor-ready models.

  4. Strengthen linkages with policymakers, investors, and ecosystem stakeholders.

  5. Enable long-term sustainability and scalability of childcare solutions.

What Makes This Challenge Unique? (Innovation Focus Areas)

The initiative emphasizes innovation across multiple dimensions:

1. Delivery and Scale Innovation

  • Community-based childcare models

  • Workplace-linked childcare solutions

  • Mobile and flexible-hour services

  • Hybrid delivery systems

2. Financing and Business Model Innovation

  • Subscription-based childcare models

  • Employer-supported childcare systems

  • Public-private partnership models

  • Voucher and cross-subsidized systems

3. Systems Integration

  • Alignment with public schemes and policies

  • Integration with employer ecosystems

  • Collaboration with local urban bodies

4. Quality Assurance and Workforce Development

  • Training and certification for childcare workers

  • Standardized safety and quality systems

  • Workforce retention strategies

5. Digital and Sustainability Innovation

  • Use of data systems and digital tools

  • Monitoring dashboards

  • Climate-resilient childcare solutions

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify for the Urban Childcare Innovation Challenge, applicants must:

  • Be women-led or women-benefiting enterprises

  • Operate within urban or peri-urban areas in India

  • Demonstrate a proof of concept (pilot or early-stage validation)

  • Address childcare needs of low-income or informal sector workers

  • Show strong potential for scalability and replication

  • Align with public or private systems integration opportunities

Program Benefits

Selected participants will gain access to a wide range of benefits:

1. Strategic Positioning

  • Clear direction for scaling and growth

  • Stronger market positioning

2. Operational Strengthening

  • Improved service delivery systems

  • Compliance and quality enhancement

3. Partnership Development

  • Connections with public, private, and community stakeholders

4. Financial Readiness

  • Investment preparedness

  • Financial sustainability planning

5. Gender-Responsive Business Models

  • Integration of gender equality principles into operations

6. Mentorship

  • Guidance from industry experts and experienced entrepreneurs

7. Capacity Development

  • Support from specialists in gender and care economy

8. Grant Support

  • Selected enterprises may receive grants of up to USD 5,000

Program Timeline

The Urban Childcare Innovation Challenge follows a structured journey:

  • 16 March 2026 – Applications Open

  • 16 April 2026 – Application Deadline

  • 20 April 2026 – Shortlisting and Cohort Announcement

  • 30 April 2026 – Launch & National Roundtable

  • 1 May – 30 July 2026 – Incubation & Mentorship Program

  • 5 August 2026 – Demo Day & Grand Finale

Program Journey Explained

Participants will go through a carefully designed process:

  1. Application and selection

  2. Onboarding and orientation

  3. Intensive incubation and mentorship

  4. Networking and stakeholder engagement

  5. Final pitch during Demo Day

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

What is the Urban Childcare Innovation Challenge?

It is an incubation program designed to support innovative childcare enterprises that improve access and quality of childcare services in urban India.

Who can apply for this program?

Women-led or women-benefiting enterprises working on childcare solutions in urban or peri-urban India.

Are early-stage startups eligible?

Yes, as long as they have a validated proof of concept or pilot.

Is financial support provided?

Yes, selected enterprises may receive grants of up to USD 5,000.

What type of support will participants receive?

Participants will receive:

  • Mentorship

  • Capacity building

  • Technical assistance

  • Networking opportunities

  • Investment readiness support

How many enterprises will be selected?

Approximately 8 to 10 enterprises will be selected.

What is the duration of the program?

The incubation program runs for approximately 3 months.

How can I apply?

Applications are submitted through the Official UN Platform during the application window.

Click HERE to learn more about the Urban Childcare Innovation Challenge 2026.

Final Thoughts

The Urban Childcare Innovation Challenge 2026 represents a powerful opportunity for women-led enterprises to transform childcare systems while scaling sustainable business solutions. By bridging innovation, gender equality, and economic empowerment, this initiative is set to redefine urban childcare across India.

Discover more global grant opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post Urban Childcare Innovation Challenge 2026: Grants, Incubation & Mentorship for Women-Led Enterprises in India appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
